title: Mercury-Atlas 8 / Sigma 7
description: Open datasets and source materials relevant to Mercury-Atlas 8 / Sigma 7 (1962), the precision engineering flight, Castor canadensis (North American Beaver), Black-headed Grosbeak (Pheucticus melanocephalus), and spacecraft resource management

sources.name: NSSDC Master Catalog -- Mercury-Atlas 8
sources.description: NASA Space Science Data Coordinated Archive entry for Mercury-Atlas 8 / Sigma 7. Spacecraft specifications (Mercury capsule #16, 1,352 kg), orbital parameters (161×283 km, 32.55° inclination, 88.9-minute period), mission timeline (six orbits, 9h 13m 11s), and recovery data (4.5 nm from USS Kearsarge). NSSDC designation 1962-052A.
sources.mission_relevance: Definitive source for Sigma 7 spacecraft specifications, orbital parameters, and mission outcomes. Documents the precision that defined the mission: 78% fuel remaining after six orbits, 4.5-mile landing accuracy, all systems within specification.

sources.name: NASA SP-12: Results of the Third United States Manned Orbital Space Flight
sources.description: The official mission report for MA-8, published by NASA's Manned Spacecraft Center in December 1962. Contains detailed systems performance data, fuel consumption profiles, attitude control mode evaluations, retrofire analysis, biomedical data, and recovery operations report. The most comprehensive engineering document for the mission.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary source for fuel consumption data (13.3 lb used, 46.7 lb remaining), control mode analysis (drift, auto, manual, FBW comparisons), retrofire accuracy, and the systems performance data that enabled MA-9 planning.

sources.name: NASA SP-45: Mercury Project Summary Including Results of the Fourth Manned Orbital Flight
sources.description: Comprehensive Mercury program summary including all orbital flight results. Contains comparative data across MA-6 through MA-9, enabling the fuel consumption and landing accuracy comparisons that define Sigma 7's contribution to the program.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides the cross-flight comparison data essential for understanding Sigma 7's significance: fuel consumption rates, landing accuracies, and systems reliability across all Mercury orbital flights.

sources.name: Jonathan McDowell's Launch Vehicle Database -- Atlas D
sources.description: Comprehensive launch vehicle database including Atlas D launch history. Atlas 113-D performance data, launch from LC-14, and orbital insertion parameters for Sigma 7.
sources.mission_relevance: Contextualizes Atlas 113-D within the Atlas D launch record and provides the launch vehicle performance data for orbital insertion calculations.

sources.name: USGS National Wetlands Inventory -- Beaver-Modified Wetlands
sources.description: National wetland inventory data including beaver-modified wetlands. GIS layers showing wetland extent, type, and modification history across the Pacific Northwest. Beaver-created ponds and associated wetlands are classified as palustrine emergent or scrub-shrub wetlands with beaver impoundment modifiers.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides the spatial data for beaver dam distribution in PNW watersheds, supporting the organism pairing analysis of Castor canadensis as ecosystem engineer.

sources.name: Washington Department of Fish and Wildlife -- Beaver Management
sources.description: WDFW species profile for Castor canadensis including population status, habitat requirements, management strategies, and beaver dam census data for Washington state. Documents the ongoing recovery of beaver populations in western Washington streams.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides regional population and habitat data for the organism pairing, with specific information about beaver activity in Snohomish County and Cascades foothills watersheds.

sources.name: eBird -- Black-headed Grosbeak Occurrence Data
sources.description: Citizen science occurrence data for Pheucticus melanocephalus across North America, including breeding season observations in Pacific Northwest mixed forests and riparian corridors. Phenology data shows arrival dates, breeding activity, and departure timing.
sources.mission_relevance: Provides the distribution and habitat context for the SPS bird at degree 24. The grosbeak's association with riparian corridors -- the same habitats where beavers build dams -- supports the ecological resonance between the organism pairing and the bird connection.

sources.name: NASA Image and Video Library -- Mercury-Atlas 8
sources.description: Historical photographs from the MA-8 mission: Sigma 7 on the launch pad at LC-14, Schirra in the spacecraft, Mission Control during the flight, recovery operations by USS Kearsarge, and post-flight spacecraft inspection. Public domain imagery.
sources.mission_relevance: Visual documentation of Sigma 7 hardware, the Atlas D launch vehicle, and recovery operations. Essential for simulation and creative arts tracks.

sources.name: Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum -- Sigma 7
sources.description: Catalog entry for the actual Sigma 7 spacecraft, currently on display at the Astronaut Hall of Fame, Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex (on loan from the Smithsonian). Dimensions, condition notes, and provenance information.
sources.mission_relevance: Physical artifact documentation. Sigma 7 is one of the best-preserved Mercury capsules, reflecting the benign mission profile -- no emergency procedures, no off-nominal events, minimal post-flight damage.

sources.name: Philo T. Farnsworth Archives -- University of Utah
sources.description: University of Utah's digital archive of Farnsworth papers, patents, photographs, and laboratory notebooks documenting the invention of electronic television. Includes the September 7, 1927 first demonstration records and the subsequent patent documentation.
sources.mission_relevance: Primary source for the dedication figure. Farnsworth's systematic scanning approach to television parallels Schirra's systematic scanning of spacecraft systems.
